a:r.a:t: The documents subsystem is the backbone of how the system ingests, compares, and reasons about uploaded files. It spans 16 Python files across extractors, comparison logic, consolidation campaigns, similarity analysis, evidence compilation, templates, and serializers. On the extraction side, it supports 9 file formats: PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, XLS, TXT, Markdown, CSV, and TSV. PDF extraction uses the Docling parser for high-fidelity parsing with a cap of 200 elements per extraction to keep context manageable. Document comparison in compare.py uses key-based matching plus fuzzy matching with a 0.72 similarity threshold. It identifies 12 obligation modality patterns - these are regulatory language patterns like "shall", "must", "required_to", "prohibited", "permitted", and "may" - each with a binding strength ranking. When obligations change between document versions, the system escalates severity automatically. Consolidation campaigns handle multi-document corpus analysis using IDF weighting, union-find clustering, and weighted Jaccard scoring across 6 focus modes. The evidence binder compiles graded evidence into formatted reference packets with inline citations - this is what the RAG pipeline uses to build its final answers. Similarity analysis uses TF-IDF fingerprinting with 28 process keyword patterns and n-gram comparison. This entire subsystem feeds directly into the RAG pipeline and the document-related tools.
